1	package workflow
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"maps"
6		"strconv"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/constants"
11		"github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/logger"
12	)
13	
14	var claudeLog = logger.New("workflow:claude_engine")
15	
16	// ClaudeEngine represents the Claude Code agentic engine
17	type ClaudeEngine struct {
18		BaseEngine
19	}
20	
21	func NewClaudeEngine() *ClaudeEngine {
22		return &ClaudeEngine{
23			BaseEngine: BaseEngine{
24				id:                       "claude",
25				displayName:              "Claude Code",
26				description:              "Uses Claude Code with full MCP tool support and allow-listing",
27				experimental:             false,
28				supportsToolsAllowlist:   true,
29				supportsMaxTurns:         true,  // Claude supports max-turns feature
30				supportsMaxContinuations: false, // Claude Code does not support --max-autopilot-continues-style continuation
31				supportsWebFetch:         true,  // Claude has built-in WebFetch support
32				supportsWebSearch:        true,  // Claude has built-in WebSearch support
33				llmGatewayPort:           constants.ClaudeLLMGatewayPort,
34			},
35		}
36	}
37	
38	// GetModelEnvVarName returns the native environment variable name that the Claude Code CLI uses
39	// for model selection. Setting ANTHROPIC_MODEL is equivalent to passing --model to the CLI.
40	func (e *ClaudeEngine) GetModelEnvVarName() string {
41		return constants.ClaudeCLIModelEnvVar
42	}
43	
44	// GetAPMTarget returns "claude" so that apm-action packs Claude-specific primitives.
45	func (e *ClaudeEngine) GetAPMTarget() string {
46		return "claude"
47	}
48	
49	// GetRequiredSecretNames returns the list of secrets required by the Claude engine
50	// This includes ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and optionally MCP_GATEWAY_API_KEY
51	func (e *ClaudeEngine) GetRequiredSecretNames(workflowData *WorkflowData) []string {
52		secrets := []string{"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"}
53	
54		// Add MCP gateway API key if MCP servers are present (gateway is always started with MCP servers)
55		if HasMCPServers(workflowData) {
56			secrets = append(secrets, "MCP_GATEWAY_API_KEY")
57		}
58	
59		// Add mcp-scripts secret names
60		if IsMCPScriptsEnabled(workflowData.MCPScripts, workflowData) {
61			mcpScriptsSecrets := collectMCPScriptsSecrets(workflowData.MCPScripts)
62			for varName := range mcpScriptsSecrets {
63				secrets = append(secrets, varName)
64			}
65		}
66	
67		return secrets
68	}
69	
70	// GetSecretValidationStep returns the secret validation step for the Claude engine.
71	// Returns an empty step if custom command is specified.
72	func (e *ClaudeEngine) GetSecretValidationStep(workflowData *WorkflowData) GitHubActionStep {
73		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Command != "" {
74			claudeLog.Printf("Skipping secret validation step: custom command specified (%s)", workflowData.EngineConfig.Command)
75			return GitHubActionStep{}
76		}
77		return GenerateMultiSecretValidationStep(
78			[]string{"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"},
79			"Claude Code",
80			"https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/engines/#anthropic-claude-code",
81			getEngineEnvOverrides(workflowData),
82		)
83	}
84	
85	func (e *ClaudeEngine) GetInstallationSteps(workflowData *WorkflowData) []GitHubActionStep {
86		claudeLog.Printf("Generating installation steps for Claude engine: workflow=%s", workflowData.Name)
87	
88		// Skip installation if custom command is specified
89		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Command != "" {
90			claudeLog.Printf("Skipping installation steps: custom command specified (%s)", workflowData.EngineConfig.Command)
91			return []GitHubActionStep{}
92		}
93	
94		var steps []GitHubActionStep
95	
96		// Define engine configuration for shared validation
97		config := EngineInstallConfig{
98			Secrets:         []string{"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"},
99			DocsURL:         "https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/engines/#anthropic-claude-code",
100			NpmPackage:      "@anthropic-ai/claude-code",
101			Version:         string(constants.DefaultClaudeCodeVersion),
102			Name:            "Claude Code",
103			CliName:         "claude",
104			InstallStepName: "Install Claude Code CLI",
105		}
106	
107		// Secret validation step is now generated in the activation job (GetSecretValidationStep).
108	
109		// Determine Claude version
110		claudeVersion := config.Version
111		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Version != "" {
112			claudeVersion = workflowData.EngineConfig.Version
113		}
114	
115		// Add Node.js setup step first (before sandbox installation)
116		npmSteps := GenerateNpmInstallSteps(
117			config.NpmPackage,
118			claudeVersion,
119			config.InstallStepName,
120			config.CliName,
121			true, // Include Node.js setup
122		)
123	
124		if len(npmSteps) > 0 {
125			steps = append(steps, npmSteps[0]) // Setup Node.js step
126		}
127	
128		// Add AWF installation if firewall is enabled
129		if isFirewallEnabled(workflowData) {
130			// Install AWF after Node.js setup but before Claude CLI installation
131			firewallConfig := getFirewallConfig(workflowData)
132			agentConfig := getAgentConfig(workflowData)
133			var awfVersion string
134			if firewallConfig != nil {
135				awfVersion = firewallConfig.Version
136			}
137	
138			// Install AWF binary (or skip if custom command is specified)
139			awfInstall := generateAWFInstallationStep(awfVersion, agentConfig)
140			if len(awfInstall) > 0 {
141				steps = append(steps, awfInstall)
142			}
143		}
144	
145		// Add Claude CLI installation step after sandbox installation
146		if len(npmSteps) > 1 {
147			steps = append(steps, npmSteps[1:]...) // Install Claude CLI and subsequent steps
148		}
149	
150		return steps
151	}
152	
153	// GetDeclaredOutputFiles returns the output files that Claude may produce
154	func (e *ClaudeEngine) GetDeclaredOutputFiles() []string {
155		return []string{}
156	}
157	
158	// GetAgentManifestFiles returns Claude-specific instruction files that should be
159	// treated as security-sensitive manifests.  Modifying CLAUDE.md can change the
160	// agent's instructions, guidelines, or permissions on the next run.
161	func (e *ClaudeEngine) GetAgentManifestFiles() []string {
162		return []string{"CLAUDE.md"}
163	}
164	
165	// GetAgentManifestPathPrefixes returns Claude-specific config directory prefixes.
166	// The .claude/ directory contains settings, custom commands, and other engine
167	// configuration that could affect agent behaviour.
168	func (e *ClaudeEngine) GetAgentManifestPathPrefixes() []string {
169		return []string{".claude/"}
170	}
171	
172	// GetExecutionSteps returns the GitHub Actions steps for executing Claude
173	func (e *ClaudeEngine) GetExecutionSteps(workflowData *WorkflowData, logFile string) []GitHubActionStep {
174		claudeLog.Printf("Generating execution steps for Claude engine: workflow=%s, firewall=%v", workflowData.Name, isFirewallEnabled(workflowData))
175	
176		var steps []GitHubActionStep
177	
178		// Build claude CLI arguments based on configuration
179		var claudeArgs []string
180	
181		// Add print flag for non-interactive mode
182		claudeArgs = append(claudeArgs, "--print")
183	
184		// Disable slash commands for controlled execution
185		claudeArgs = append(claudeArgs, "--disable-slash-commands")
186	
187		// Disable Chrome integration for security and deterministic execution
188		claudeArgs = append(claudeArgs, "--no-chrome")
189	
190		// Model is always passed via the native ANTHROPIC_MODEL environment variable when configured.
191		// This avoids embedding the value directly in the shell command (which fails template injection
192		// validation for GitHub Actions expressions like ${{ inputs.model }}).
193		// Fallback for unconfigured model uses GH_AW_MODEL_AGENT_CLAUDE with shell expansion.
194		modelConfigured := workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Model != ""
195	
196		// Add max_turns if specified (in CLI it's max-turns)
197		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.MaxTurns != "" {
198			claudeLog.Printf("Setting max turns: %s", workflowData.EngineConfig.MaxTurns)
199			claudeArgs = append(claudeArgs, "--max-turns", workflowData.EngineConfig.MaxTurns)
200		}
201	
202		// Add MCP configuration only if there are MCP servers
203		if HasMCPServers(workflowData) {
204			claudeLog.Print("Adding MCP configuration")
205			claudeArgs = append(claudeArgs, "--mcp-config", "/tmp/gh-aw/mcp-config/mcp-servers.json")
206		}
207	
208		// Add allowed tools configuration
209		// Note: Claude Code CLI v2.0.31 introduced a simpler --tools flag, but we continue to use
210		// --allowed-tools because it provides fine-grained control needed by gh-aw:
211		// - Specific bash commands: Bash(git:*), Bash(ls)
212		// - MCP tool prefixes: mcp__github__issue_read
213		// - Path-specific tools: Read(/tmp/gh-aw/cache-memory/*)
214		// The --tools flag only supports basic tool names (e.g., "Bash,Edit,Read") without patterns.
215		allowedTools := e.computeAllowedClaudeToolsString(workflowData.Tools, workflowData.SafeOutputs, workflowData.CacheMemoryConfig)
216		if allowedTools != "" {
217			claudeArgs = append(claudeArgs, "--allowed-tools", allowedTools)
218		}
219	
220		// Add debug-file flag to write debug logs directly to file
221		// This implicitly enables debug mode and provides cleaner, more reliable log capture
222		// than shell redirection with 2>&1 | tee
223		claudeArgs = append(claudeArgs, "--debug-file", logFile)
224	
225		// Always add verbose flag for enhanced debugging output
226		claudeArgs = append(claudeArgs, "--verbose")
227	
228		// Add permission mode for non-interactive execution (bypass permissions)
229		claudeArgs = append(claudeArgs, "--permission-mode", "bypassPermissions")
230	
231		// Add output format for structured output
232		// Use "stream-json" to output JSONL format (newline-delimited JSON objects)
233		// This format is compatible with the log parser which expects either JSON array or JSONL
234		claudeArgs = append(claudeArgs, "--output-format", "stream-json")
235	
236		// Add custom args from engine configuration before the prompt
237		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && len(workflowData.EngineConfig.Args) > 0 {
238			claudeArgs = append(claudeArgs, workflowData.EngineConfig.Args...)
239		}
240	
241		// Build the agent command - prepend custom agent file content if specified (via imports)
242		var promptSetup string
243		var promptCommand string
244		if workflowData.AgentFile != "" {
245			agentPath, err := ResolveAgentFilePath(workflowData.AgentFile)
246			if err != nil {
247				claudeLog.Printf("Error resolving agent file path: %v", err)
248				return BuildInvalidAgentPathStep("Execute Claude Code CLI", workflowData.AgentFile, err)
249			}
250			claudeLog.Printf("Using custom agent file: %s", workflowData.AgentFile)
251			// Extract markdown body from custom agent file and prepend to prompt
252			promptSetup = fmt.Sprintf(`# Extract markdown body from custom agent file (skip frontmatter)
253	          AGENT_CONTENT="$(awk 'BEGIN{skip=1} /^---$/{if(skip){skip=0;next}else{skip=1;next}} !skip' %s)"
254	          # Combine agent content with prompt
255	          PROMPT_TEXT="$(printf '%%s\n\n%%s' "$AGENT_CONTENT" "$(cat /tmp/gh-aw/aw-prompts/prompt.txt)")"`, agentPath)
256			promptCommand = `"$PROMPT_TEXT"`
257		} else {
258			promptCommand = `"$(cat /tmp/gh-aw/aw-prompts/prompt.txt)"`
259		}
260	
261		// Build the command string with proper argument formatting
262		// Determine which command to use
263		var commandName string
264		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Command != "" {
265			commandName = workflowData.EngineConfig.Command
266			claudeLog.Printf("Using custom command: %s", commandName)
267		} else {
268			// Use regular claude command - PATH is inherited via --env-all in AWF mode
269			commandName = "claude"
270		}
271	
272		commandParts := []string{commandName}
273		commandParts = append(commandParts, claudeArgs...)
274	
275		// Join command parts (excluding the prompt) with proper escaping.
276		// The prompt command is appended raw after shellJoinArgs because it contains
277		// shell variable references ("$PROMPT_TEXT", "$(cat ...)") that must NOT be
278		// escaped — single-quoting them would prevent shell expansion at runtime.
279		claudeCommand := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s", shellJoinArgs(commandParts), promptCommand)
280	
281		// When model is not configured, use the GH_AW_MODEL_AGENT_CLAUDE fallback env var
282		// via shell expansion so users can set a default via GitHub Actions variables.
283		// When model IS configured, ANTHROPIC_MODEL is set in the env block (see below) and the
284		// Claude CLI reads it natively - no --model flag in the shell command needed.
285		if !modelConfigured {
286			isDetectionJob := workflowData.SafeOutputs == nil
287			var modelEnvVar string
288			if isDetectionJob {
289				modelEnvVar = constants.EnvVarModelDetectionClaude
290			} else {
291				modelEnvVar = constants.EnvVarModelAgentClaude
292			}
293			claudeCommand = fmt.Sprintf(`%s${%s:+ --model "$%s"}`, claudeCommand, modelEnvVar, modelEnvVar)
294		}
295	
296		// Build the full command based on whether firewall is enabled
297		var command string
298		if isFirewallEnabled(workflowData) {
299			// Build the AWF-wrapped command using helper function
300			// Get allowed domains (Claude defaults + network permissions + HTTP MCP server URLs + runtime ecosystem domains)
301			allowedDomains := GetClaudeAllowedDomainsWithToolsAndRuntimes(workflowData.NetworkPermissions, workflowData.Tools, workflowData.Runtimes)
302			// Add GHES/custom API target domains to the firewall allow-list when engine.api-target is set
303			if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.APITarget != "" {
304				allowedDomains = mergeAPITargetDomains(allowedDomains, workflowData.EngineConfig.APITarget)
305			}
306	
307			// Build AWF command with all configuration
308			// AWF v0.15.0+ uses chroot mode by default, providing transparent access to host binaries
309			// AWF with --enable-chroot and --env-all handles most PATH setup natively:
310			// - GOROOT, JAVA_HOME, etc. are handled via AWF_HOST_PATH and entrypoint.sh
311			// However, npm-installed CLIs (like claude) need hostedtoolcache bin directories in PATH.
312			// We prepend GetNpmBinPathSetup() to the engine command so it runs inside the AWF container.
313			npmPathSetup := GetNpmBinPathSetup()
314			claudeCommandWithPath := fmt.Sprintf(`%s && %s`, npmPathSetup, claudeCommand)
315	
316			// Build host-side path setup: create the agent step summary file so it is accessible
317			// inside the sandbox. Combine with any existing promptSetup (may be empty).
318			touchSummary := "touch " + AgentStepSummaryPath
319			hostSetup := touchSummary
320			if promptSetup != "" {
321				hostSetup = promptSetup + "\n" + touchSummary
322			}
323	
324			// Note: Claude Code CLI writes debug logs to --debug-file and JSON output to stdout
325			// Use tee to capture stdout (stream-json output) to the log file while also displaying on console
326			// The combined output (debug logs + JSON) will be in the log file for parsing
327			command = BuildAWFCommand(AWFCommandConfig{
328				EngineName:     "claude",
329				EngineCommand:  claudeCommandWithPath, // Command with npm PATH setup runs inside AWF
330				LogFile:        logFile,
331				WorkflowData:   workflowData,
332				UsesTTY:        true, // Claude Code CLI requires TTY
333				AllowedDomains: allowedDomains,
334				PathSetup:      hostSetup, // Runs BEFORE AWF on the host (prompt setup + summary file creation)
335				// Exclude every env var whose step-env value is a secret so the agent
336				// cannot read raw token values via bash tools (env / printenv).
337				ExcludeEnvVarNames: ComputeAWFExcludeEnvVarNames(workflowData, []string{"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"}),
338			})
339		} else {
340			// Run Claude command without AWF wrapper
341			// Note: Claude Code CLI writes debug logs to --debug-file and JSON output to stdout
342			// Use tee to capture stdout (stream-json output) to the log file while also displaying on console
343			// The combined output (debug logs + JSON) will be in the log file for parsing
344			// PATH is already set correctly by actions/setup-* steps which prepend to PATH
345			if promptSetup != "" {
346				command = fmt.Sprintf(`set -o pipefail
347	          touch %s
348	          %s
349	          # Execute Claude Code CLI with prompt from file
350	          %s 2>&1 | tee -a %s`, AgentStepSummaryPath, promptSetup, claudeCommand, logFile)
351			} else {
352				command = fmt.Sprintf(`set -o pipefail
353	          touch %s
354	          # Execute Claude Code CLI with prompt from file
355	          %s 2>&1 | tee -a %s`, AgentStepSummaryPath, claudeCommand, logFile)
356			}
357		}
358	
359		// Build environment variables map
360		env := map[string]string{
361			"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY":       "${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}",
362			"DISABLE_TELEMETRY":       "1",
363			"DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING": "1",
364			"DISABLE_BUG_COMMAND":     "1",
365			"GH_AW_PROMPT":            "/tmp/gh-aw/aw-prompts/prompt.txt",
366			// Tag the step as a GitHub AW agentic execution for discoverability by agents
367			"GITHUB_AW": "true",
368			// Override GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY with a path that exists inside the sandbox.
369			// The runner's original path is unreachable within the AWF isolated filesystem;
370			// we create this file before the agent starts and append it to the real
371			// $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY after secret redaction.
372			"GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY": AgentStepSummaryPath,
373			"GITHUB_WORKSPACE":    "${{ github.workspace }}",
374		}
375		// Indicate the phase: "agent" for the main run, "detection" for threat detection
376		// Include the compiler version so agents can identify which gh-aw version generated the workflow
377		if workflowData.IsDetectionRun {
378			env["GH_AW_PHASE"] = "detection"
379		} else {
380			env["GH_AW_PHASE"] = "agent"
381		}
382		if IsRelease() {
383			env["GH_AW_VERSION"] = GetVersion()
384		} else {
385			env["GH_AW_VERSION"] = "dev"
386		}
387	
388		// Add GH_AW_MCP_CONFIG for MCP server configuration only if there are MCP servers
389		if HasMCPServers(workflowData) {
390			env["GH_AW_MCP_CONFIG"] = "/tmp/gh-aw/mcp-config/mcp-servers.json"
391		}
392	
393		// In sandbox (AWF) mode, set git identity environment variables so the first git commit
394		// succeeds inside the container. AWF's --env-all forwards these to the container, ensuring
395		// git does not rely on the host-side ~/.gitconfig which is not visible in the sandbox.
396		if isFirewallEnabled(workflowData) {
397			maps.Copy(env, getGitIdentityEnvVars())
398		}
399	
400		// Set timeout environment variables for Claude Code
401		// Use tools.startup-timeout if specified, otherwise default to DefaultMCPStartupTimeout
402		startupTimeoutMs := int(constants.DefaultMCPStartupTimeout / time.Millisecond)
403		if workflowData.ToolsStartupTimeout > 0 {
404			startupTimeoutMs = workflowData.ToolsStartupTimeout * 1000 // convert seconds to milliseconds
405		}
406	
407		// Use tools.timeout if specified, otherwise default to DefaultToolTimeout
408		timeoutMs := int(constants.DefaultToolTimeout / time.Millisecond)
409		if workflowData.ToolsTimeout > 0 {
410			timeoutMs = workflowData.ToolsTimeout * 1000 // convert seconds to milliseconds
411		}
412	
413		env["MCP_TIMEOUT"] = strconv.Itoa(startupTimeoutMs)
414		env["MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT"] = strconv.Itoa(timeoutMs)
415		env["BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS"] = strconv.Itoa(timeoutMs)
416		env["BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS"] = strconv.Itoa(timeoutMs)
417	
418		// Add GH_AW_SAFE_OUTPUTS if output is needed
419		applySafeOutputEnvToMap(env, workflowData)
420	
421		// Add GH_AW_STARTUP_TIMEOUT environment variable (in seconds) if startup-timeout is specified
422		if workflowData.ToolsStartupTimeout > 0 {
423			env["GH_AW_STARTUP_TIMEOUT"] = strconv.Itoa(workflowData.ToolsStartupTimeout)
424		}
425	
426		// Add GH_AW_TOOL_TIMEOUT environment variable (in seconds) if timeout is specified
427		if workflowData.ToolsTimeout > 0 {
428			env["GH_AW_TOOL_TIMEOUT"] = strconv.Itoa(workflowData.ToolsTimeout)
429		}
430	
431		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.MaxTurns != "" {
432			env["GH_AW_MAX_TURNS"] = workflowData.EngineConfig.MaxTurns
433		}
434	
435		// Set the model environment variable.
436		// When model is configured, use the native ANTHROPIC_MODEL env var - the Claude CLI reads it
437		// directly, avoiding the need to embed the value in the shell command (which would fail
438		// template injection validation for GitHub Actions expressions like ${{ inputs.model }}).
439		// When model is not configured, fall back to GH_AW_MODEL_AGENT/DETECTION_CLAUDE so users
440		// can set a default via GitHub Actions variables.
441		if modelConfigured {
442			claudeLog.Printf("Setting %s env var for model: %s", constants.ClaudeCLIModelEnvVar, workflowData.EngineConfig.Model)
443			env[constants.ClaudeCLIModelEnvVar] = workflowData.EngineConfig.Model
444		} else {
445			// No model configured - use fallback GitHub variable with shell expansion
446			isDetectionJob := workflowData.SafeOutputs == nil
447			if isDetectionJob {
448				env[constants.EnvVarModelDetectionClaude] = fmt.Sprintf("${{ vars.%s || '' }}", constants.EnvVarModelDetectionClaude)
449			} else {
450				env[constants.EnvVarModelAgentClaude] = fmt.Sprintf("${{ vars.%s || '' }}", constants.EnvVarModelAgentClaude)
451			}
452		}
453	
454		// Add custom environment variables from engine config
455		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && len(workflowData.EngineConfig.Env) > 0 {
456			maps.Copy(env, workflowData.EngineConfig.Env)
457		}
458	
459		// Add custom environment variables from agent config
460		agentConfig := getAgentConfig(workflowData)
461		if agentConfig != nil && len(agentConfig.Env) > 0 {
462			maps.Copy(env, agentConfig.Env)
463			claudeLog.Printf("Added %d custom env vars from agent config", len(agentConfig.Env))
464		}
465	
466		// Add mcp-scripts secrets to env for passthrough to MCP servers
467		if IsMCPScriptsEnabled(workflowData.MCPScripts, workflowData) {
468			mcpScriptsSecrets := collectMCPScriptsSecrets(workflowData.MCPScripts)
469			for varName, secretExpr := range mcpScriptsSecrets {
470				// Only add if not already in env
471				if _, exists := env[varName]; !exists {
472					env[varName] = secretExpr
473				}
474			}
475		}
476	
477		// Generate the step for Claude CLI execution
478		stepName := "Execute Claude Code CLI"
479		var stepLines []string
480	
481		stepLines = append(stepLines, "      - name: "+stepName)
482		stepLines = append(stepLines, "        id: agentic_execution")
483	
484		// Add allowed tools comment before the run section
485		allowedToolsComment := e.generateAllowedToolsComment(e.computeAllowedClaudeToolsString(workflowData.Tools, workflowData.SafeOutputs, workflowData.CacheMemoryConfig), "        ")
486		if allowedToolsComment != "" {
487			// Split the comment into lines and add each line
488			commentLines := strings.Split(strings.TrimSuffix(allowedToolsComment, "\n"), "\n")
489			stepLines = append(stepLines, commentLines...)
490		}
491	
492		// Add timeout at step level (GitHub Actions standard)
493		if workflowData.TimeoutMinutes != "" {
494			// Strip timeout-minutes prefix
495			timeoutValue := strings.TrimPrefix(workflowData.TimeoutMinutes, "timeout-minutes: ")
496			stepLines = append(stepLines, "        timeout-minutes: "+timeoutValue)
497		} else {
498			stepLines = append(stepLines, fmt.Sprintf("        timeout-minutes: %d", int(constants.DefaultAgenticWorkflowTimeout/time.Minute))) // Default timeout for agentic workflows
499		}
500	
501		// Filter environment variables to only include allowed secrets
502		// This is a security measure to prevent exposing unnecessary secrets to the AWF container
503		allowedSecrets := e.GetRequiredSecretNames(workflowData)
504		filteredEnv := FilterEnvForSecrets(env, allowedSecrets)
505	
506		// Format step with command and filtered environment variables using shared helper
507		stepLines = FormatStepWithCommandAndEnv(stepLines, command, filteredEnv)
508	
509		steps = append(steps, GitHubActionStep(stepLines))
510	
511		return steps
512	}

155	// GetExecutionSteps returns the GitHub Actions steps for executing Codex
156	func (e *CodexEngine) GetExecutionSteps(workflowData *WorkflowData, logFile string) []GitHubActionStep {
157		modelConfigured := workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Model != ""
158		firewallEnabled := isFirewallEnabled(workflowData)
159		codexEngineLog.Printf("Building Codex execution steps: workflow=%s, modelConfigured=%v, has_agent_file=%v, firewall=%v",

161	
162		var steps []GitHubActionStep
163	
164		// Codex does not support a native model environment variable, so model selection
165		// always uses GH_AW_MODEL_AGENT_CODEX or GH_AW_MODEL_DETECTION_CODEX with shell expansion.

167		isDetectionJob := workflowData.SafeOutputs == nil
168		var modelEnvVar string
169		if isDetectionJob {
170			modelEnvVar = constants.EnvVarModelDetectionCodex
171		} else {
172			modelEnvVar = constants.EnvVarModelAgentCodex
173		}
174		modelParam := fmt.Sprintf(`${%s:+-c model="$%s" }`, modelEnvVar, modelEnvVar)
175	
176		// Build search parameter: disable web search by default, enable only if web-search tool is present.
177		// Codex enables web search by default, so we must explicitly set web_search="disabled" to disable it.
178		// The --no-search flag does not exist; use the -c web_search="disabled" config option instead.
179		// See https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli/features#web-search
180		// Leading space is intentional: the format string concatenates this directly after "exec" with no space separator.
181		webSearchParam := ` -c web_search="disabled"`

190		// --skip-git-repo-check: Allows running in directories without a git repo
191		fullAutoParam := " --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --skip-git-repo-check "
192	
193		// Build custom args parameter if specified in engineConfig
194		var customArgsParam string
195		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && len(workflowData.EngineConfig.Args) > 0 {
196			var customArgsParamSb strings.Builder
197			for _, arg := range workflowData.EngineConfig.Args {

204		// Determine which command to use

209		} else {

211			commandName = "codex"

218		var command string

284	touch %s
285	INSTRUCTION="$(cat "$GH_AW_PROMPT")"
286	mkdir -p "$CODEX_HOME/logs"
287	%s %sexec%s%s%s"$INSTRUCTION" 2>&1 | tee %s`, AgentStepSummaryPath, commandName, modelParam, webSearchParam, fullAutoParam, customArgsParam, logFile)

291		// Get effective GitHub token based on precedence: custom token > default

301			"GH_AW_PROMPT":        "/tmp/gh-aw/aw-prompts/prompt.txt",
302			// Tag the step as a GitHub AW agentic execution for discoverability by agents
303			"GITHUB_AW":                    "true",
304			"GH_AW_MCP_CONFIG":             "/tmp/gh-aw/mcp-config/config.toml",
305			"CODEX_HOME":                   "/tmp/gh-aw/mcp-config",
306			"RUST_LOG":                     "trace,hyper_util=info,mio=info,reqwest=info,os_info=info,codex_otel=warn,codex_core=debug,ocodex_exec=debug",

308			"GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN": effectiveGitHubToken,                                     // Used by GitHub MCP server via env_vars
309			"OPENAI_API_KEY":               "${{ secrets.CODEX_API_KEY || secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}", // Fallback for CODEX_API_KEY

312		// Include the compiler version so agents can identify which gh-aw version generated the workflow
313		if workflowData.IsDetectionRun {
314			env["GH_AW_PHASE"] = "detection"
315		} else {
316			env["GH_AW_PHASE"] = "agent"
317		}
318		if IsRelease() {
319			env["GH_AW_VERSION"] = GetVersion()
320		} else {
321			env["GH_AW_VERSION"] = "dev"
322		}
323	
324		// Add GH_AW_SAFE_OUTPUTS if output is needed
325		applySafeOutputEnvToMap(env, workflowData)
326	
327		// In sandbox (AWF) mode, set git identity environment variables so the first git commit
328		// succeeds inside the container. AWF's --env-all forwards these to the container, ensuring
329		// git does not rely on the host-side ~/.gitconfig which is not visible in the sandbox.
330		if firewallEnabled {
331			maps.Copy(env, getGitIdentityEnvVars())
332		}
333	
334		// Add GH_AW_STARTUP_TIMEOUT environment variable (in seconds) if startup-timeout is specified
335		if workflowData.ToolsStartupTimeout > 0 {
336			env["GH_AW_STARTUP_TIMEOUT"] = strconv.Itoa(workflowData.ToolsStartupTimeout)
337		}
338	
339		// Add GH_AW_TOOL_TIMEOUT environment variable (in seconds) if timeout is specified
340		if workflowData.ToolsTimeout > 0 {
341			env["GH_AW_TOOL_TIMEOUT"] = strconv.Itoa(workflowData.ToolsTimeout)
342		}
343	
344		// Set the model environment variable.
345		// Codex has no native model env var, so model selection always goes through
346		// GH_AW_MODEL_AGENT_CODEX / GH_AW_MODEL_DETECTION_CODEX with shell expansion.
347		// When model is configured (static or GitHub Actions expression), set the env var directly.
348		// When not configured, use the GitHub variable fallback so users can set a default.
349		if modelConfigured {
350			codexEngineLog.Printf("Setting %s env var for model: %s", modelEnvVar, workflowData.EngineConfig.Model)
351			env[modelEnvVar] = workflowData.EngineConfig.Model
352		} else {
353			env[modelEnvVar] = fmt.Sprintf("${{ vars.%s || '' }}", modelEnvVar)
354		}
355	
356		// Add custom environment variables from engine config
357		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && len(workflowData.EngineConfig.Env) > 0 {
358			maps.Copy(env, workflowData.EngineConfig.Env)
359		}
360	
361		// Add custom environment variables from agent config
362		agentConfig := getAgentConfig(workflowData)
363		if agentConfig != nil && len(agentConfig.Env) > 0 {
364			maps.Copy(env, agentConfig.Env)
365			codexEngineLog.Printf("Added %d custom env vars from agent config", len(agentConfig.Env))
366		}
367	
368		// Add mcp-scripts secrets to env for passthrough to MCP servers
369		if IsMCPScriptsEnabled(workflowData.MCPScripts, workflowData) {
370			mcpScriptsSecrets := collectMCPScriptsSecrets(workflowData.MCPScripts)
371			for varName, secretExpr := range mcpScriptsSecrets {
372				// Only add if not already in env
373				if _, exists := env[varName]; !exists {
374					env[varName] = secretExpr
375				}
376			}
377		}
378	
379		// Generate the step for Codex execution
380		stepName := "Execute Codex CLI"
381		var stepLines []string
382	
383		stepLines = append(stepLines, "      - name: "+stepName)
384		stepLines = append(stepLines, "        id: agentic_execution")
385	
386		// Filter environment variables to only include allowed secrets
387		// This is a security measure to prevent exposing unnecessary secrets to the AWF container
388		allowedSecrets := e.GetRequiredSecretNames(workflowData)
389		filteredEnv := FilterEnvForSecrets(env, allowedSecrets)
390	
391		// Format step with command and filtered environment variables using shared helper
392		stepLines = FormatStepWithCommandAndEnv(stepLines, command, filteredEnv)
393	
394		steps = append(steps, GitHubActionStep(stepLines))
395	
396		return steps
397	}

424		// Create a copy of the tools config

428			WebFetch:         toolsConfig.WebFetch,
429			WebSearch:        toolsConfig.WebSearch,

433			CacheMemory:      toolsConfig.CacheMemory,
434			Timeout:          toolsConfig.Timeout,
435			StartupTimeout:   toolsConfig.StartupTimeout,
436			Custom:           make(map[string]MCPServerConfig),
437			raw:              make(map[string]any),
438		}
439	
440		// Copy custom tools
441		maps.Copy(result.Custom, toolsConfig.Custom)
442	
443		// Copy raw map
444		maps.Copy(result.raw, toolsConfig.raw)
445	
446		// Handle playwright tool by converting it to an MCP tool configuration with copilot agent tools
447		if toolsConfig.Playwright != nil {
448			// Create an updated Playwright config with the allowed tools
449			playwrightConfig := &PlaywrightToolConfig{
450				Version: toolsConfig.Playwright.Version,
451				Args:    toolsConfig.Playwright.Args,
452			}
453	
454			result.Playwright = playwrightConfig
455	
456			// Also update the Custom map entry for playwright with allowed tools list
457			playwrightMCP := map[string]any{
458				"allowed": GetPlaywrightTools(),
459			}
460			if playwrightConfig.Version != "" {
461				playwrightMCP["version"] = playwrightConfig.Version
462			}
463			if len(playwrightConfig.Args) > 0 {
464				playwrightMCP["args"] = playwrightConfig.Args
465			}
466	
467			// Update raw map for backward compatibility
468			result.raw["playwright"] = playwrightMCP
469		}
470	
471		return result

482	// renderShellEnvironmentPolicy generates the [shell_environment_policy] section for config.toml
483	// This controls which environment variables are passed through to MCP servers for security
484	func (e *CodexEngine) renderShellEnvironmentPolicy(yaml *strings.Builder, tools map[string]any, mcpTools []string, workflowData *WorkflowData) {
485		// Collect all environment variables needed by MCP servers
486		envVars := make(map[string]bool)
487	
488		// Always include core environment variables
489		envVars["PATH"] = true
490		envVars["HOME"] = true
491	
492		// Add CODEX_API_KEY for authentication
493		envVars["CODEX_API_KEY"] = true
494		envVars["OPENAI_API_KEY"] = true // Fallback for CODEX_API_KEY
495	
496		// Check each MCP tool for required environment variables
497		for _, toolName := range mcpTools {
498			switch toolName {
499			case "github":
500				// GitHub MCP server needs GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN
501				envVars["GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN"] = true
502			case "agentic-workflows":
503				// Agentic workflows MCP server needs GITHUB_TOKEN
504				envVars["GITHUB_TOKEN"] = true
505			case "safe-outputs":
506				// Safe outputs MCP server needs several environment variables
507				envVars["GH_AW_SAFE_OUTPUTS"] = true
508				envVars["GH_AW_ASSETS_BRANCH"] = true
509				envVars["GH_AW_ASSETS_MAX_SIZE_KB"] = true
510				envVars["GH_AW_ASSETS_ALLOWED_EXTS"] = true
511				envVars["GITHUB_REPOSITORY"] = true
512				envVars["GITHUB_SERVER_URL"] = true
513			default:
514				// For custom MCP tools, check if they have env configuration
515				if toolValue, ok := tools[toolName]; ok {
516					if toolConfig, ok := toolValue.(map[string]any); ok {
517						// Extract environment variable names from env configuration
518						if env, hasEnv := toolConfig["env"].(map[string]any); hasEnv {
519							for envKey := range env {
520								envVars[envKey] = true
521							}
522						}
523					}
524				}
525			}
526		}
527	
528		// Sort environment variable names for consistent output
529		var sortedEnvVars []string
530		for envVar := range envVars {
531			sortedEnvVars = append(sortedEnvVars, envVar)
532		}
533		sort.Strings(sortedEnvVars)
534	
535		// Render [shell_environment_policy] section
536		yaml.WriteString("          \n")
537		yaml.WriteString("          [shell_environment_policy]\n")
538		yaml.WriteString("          inherit = \"core\"\n")
539		yaml.WriteString("          include_only = [")
540		for i, envVar := range sortedEnvVars {
541			if i > 0 {
542				yaml.WriteString(", ")
543			}
544			yaml.WriteString("\"" + envVar + "\"")
545		}
546		yaml.WriteString("]\n")
547	}

2	//
3	// This file contains functions for generating GitHub Actions steps to install
4	// the GitHub Copilot CLI and related sandbox infrastructure (AWF or SRT).
5	//
6	// Installation order:
7	//  1. Secret validation (COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN) — runs in the activation job
8	//  2. Node.js setup
9	//  3. Sandbox installation (SRT or AWF, if needed)
10	//  4. Copilot CLI installation
11	//
12	// The installation strategy differs based on sandbox mode:
13	//   - Standard mode: Global installation using official installer script
14	//   - SRT mode: Local npm installation for offline compatibility
15	//   - AWF mode: Global installation + AWF binary

17	package workflow
18	
19	import (
20		"github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/constants"

22	)

24	var copilotInstallLog = logger.New("workflow:copilot_engine_installation")

26	// GetSecretValidationStep returns the secret validation step for the Copilot engine.
27	// Returns an empty step if copilot-requests feature is enabled or custom command is specified.
28	func (e *CopilotEngine) GetSecretValidationStep(workflowData *WorkflowData) GitHubActionStep {
29		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Command != "" {
30			copilotInstallLog.Printf("Skipping secret validation step: custom command specified (%s)", workflowData.EngineConfig.Command)
31			return GitHubActionStep{}
32		}
33		if isFeatureEnabled(constants.CopilotRequestsFeatureFlag, workflowData) {
34			copilotInstallLog.Print("Skipping secret validation step: copilot-requests feature enabled, using GitHub Actions token")
35			return GitHubActionStep{}
36		}
37		return GenerateMultiSecretValidationStep(
38			[]string{"COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN"},
39			"GitHub Copilot CLI",
40			"https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/engines/#github-copilot-default",
41			getEngineEnvOverrides(workflowData),
42		)
43	}

45	// GetInstallationSteps generates the complete installation workflow for Copilot CLI.
46	// This includes Node.js setup, sandbox installation (SRT or AWF), and Copilot CLI installation.
47	// Secret validation is handled separately in the activation job via GetSecretValidationStep.
48	// The installation order is:
49	// 1. Node.js setup
50	// 2. Sandbox installation (SRT or AWF, if needed)
51	// 3. Copilot CLI installation
52	//
53	// If a custom command is specified in the engine configuration, this function returns
54	// an empty list of steps, skipping the standard installation process.
55	func (e *CopilotEngine) GetInstallationSteps(workflowData *WorkflowData) []GitHubActionStep {
56		copilotInstallLog.Printf("Generating installation steps for Copilot engine: workflow=%s", workflowData.Name)
57	
58		// Skip installation if custom command is specified
59		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Command != "" {
60			copilotInstallLog.Printf("Skipping installation steps: custom command specified (%s)", workflowData.EngineConfig.Command)
61			return []GitHubActionStep{}
62		}
63	
64		var steps []GitHubActionStep
65	
66		// Define engine configuration for shared validation
67		config := EngineInstallConfig{
68			Secrets:         []string{"COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN"},
69			DocsURL:         "https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/engines/#github-copilot-default",
70			NpmPackage:      "@github/copilot",
71			Version:         string(constants.DefaultCopilotVersion),
72			Name:            "GitHub Copilot CLI",
73			CliName:         "copilot",
74			InstallStepName: "Install GitHub Copilot CLI",
75		}
76	
77		// Secret validation step is now generated in the activation job (GetSecretValidationStep).
78	
79		// Determine Copilot version
80		copilotVersion := config.Version
81		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Version != "" {
82			copilotVersion = workflowData.EngineConfig.Version
83		}
84	
85		// Determine if Copilot should be installed globally
86		// Always install globally now (SRT removed)
87		installGlobally := true
88	
89		// Generate install steps based on installation scope
90		var npmSteps []GitHubActionStep
91		if installGlobally {
92			// Use the new installer script for global installation
93			copilotInstallLog.Print("Using new installer script for Copilot installation")
94			npmSteps = GenerateCopilotInstallerSteps(copilotVersion, config.InstallStepName)
95		}
96	
97		// Add Node.js setup step first (before sandbox installation)
98		if len(npmSteps) > 0 {
99			steps = append(steps, npmSteps[0]) // Setup Node.js step
100		}
101	
102		// Add sandbox installation steps (AWF only)
103		if isFirewallEnabled(workflowData) {
104			// Install AWF after Node.js setup but before Copilot CLI installation
105			firewallConfig := getFirewallConfig(workflowData)
106			agentConfig := getAgentConfig(workflowData)
107			var awfVersion string
108			if firewallConfig != nil {
109				awfVersion = firewallConfig.Version
110			}
111	
112			// Install AWF binary (or skip if custom command is specified)
113			awfInstall := generateAWFInstallationStep(awfVersion, agentConfig)
114			if len(awfInstall) > 0 {
115				steps = append(steps, awfInstall)
116			}
117		}
118	
119		// Add Copilot CLI installation step after sandbox installation
120		if len(npmSteps) > 1 {
121			steps = append(steps, npmSteps[1:]...) // Install Copilot CLI and subsequent steps
122		}
123	
124		return steps
125	}

127	// generateAWFInstallationStep creates a GitHub Actions step to install the AWF binary
128	// with SHA256 checksum verification to protect against supply chain attacks.
129	//
130	// The installation logic is implemented in a separate shell script (install_awf_binary.sh)
131	// which downloads the binary directly from GitHub releases, verifies its checksum against
132	// the official checksums.txt file, and installs it. This approach:
133	// - Eliminates trust in the installer script itself
134	// - Provides full transparency of the installation process
135	// - Protects against tampered or compromised installer scripts
136	// - Verifies the binary integrity before execution
137	//
138	// If a custom command is specified in the agent config, the installation is skipped
139	// as the custom command replaces the AWF binary.
140	func generateAWFInstallationStep(version string, agentConfig *AgentSandboxConfig) GitHubActionStep {
141		// If custom command is specified, skip installation (command replaces binary)
142		if agentConfig != nil && agentConfig.Command != "" {
143			copilotInstallLog.Print("Skipping AWF binary installation (custom command specified)")
144			// Return empty step - custom command will be used in execution
145			return GitHubActionStep([]string{})
146		}
147	
148		// Use default version for logging when not specified
149		if version == "" {
150			version = string(constants.DefaultFirewallVersion)
151		}
152	
153		stepLines := []string{
154			"      - name: Install AWF binary",
155			"        run: bash ${RUNNER_TEMP}/gh-aw/actions/install_awf_binary.sh " + version,
156		}
157	
158		return GitHubActionStep(stepLines)
159	}

165	// GetDeclaredOutputFiles returns the output files that Gemini may produce.
166	// Gemini CLI writes structured error reports to /tmp/gemini-client-error-*.json
167	// with a timestamp in the filename (e.g. gemini-client-error-Turn.run-sendMessageStream-2026-02-21T20-45-59-824Z.json).
168	// These files provide detailed diagnostics when the Gemini API call fails.
169	// GetPreBundleSteps moves these files into /tmp/gh-aw/ so all artifact paths share a common
170	// ancestor under /tmp/gh-aw/ and the actions/upload-artifact LCA calculation stays correct.
171	func (e *GeminiEngine) GetDeclaredOutputFiles() []string {
172		return []string{
173			"/tmp/gh-aw/gemini-client-error-*.json",
174		}
175	}
176	
177	// GetPreBundleSteps returns a step that moves Gemini CLI error reports from /tmp/ into
178	// /tmp/gh-aw/ before the unified artifact upload. This keeps all artifact paths under
179	// /tmp/gh-aw/ so that actions/upload-artifact computes the correct least-common-ancestor
180	// path and downstream jobs find files at the expected locations.
181	func (e *GeminiEngine) GetPreBundleSteps(workflowData *WorkflowData) []GitHubActionStep {
182		return []GitHubActionStep{
183			{
184				"      - name: Move Gemini error files to artifact directory",
185				"        if: always()",
186				"        run: mv /tmp/gemini-client-error-*.json /tmp/gh-aw/ 2>/dev/null || true",
187			},
188		}
189	}
190	
191	// GetExecutionSteps returns the GitHub Actions steps for executing Gemini
192	func (e *GeminiEngine) GetExecutionSteps(workflowData *WorkflowData, logFile string) []GitHubActionStep {
193		geminiLog.Printf("Generating execution steps for Gemini engine: workflow=%s, firewall=%v", workflowData.Name, isFirewallEnabled(workflowData))
194	
195		var steps []GitHubActionStep
196	
197		// Write .gemini/settings.json with context.includeDirectories and tools.core.
198		// This step runs after the MCP gateway setup (which may have written mcpServers config)
199		// and merges the context/tools settings into any existing settings.json.
200		settingsStep := e.generateGeminiSettingsStep(workflowData)
201		steps = append(steps, settingsStep)
202	
203		// Build gemini CLI arguments based on configuration
204		var geminiArgs []string
205	
206		// Model is passed via the native GEMINI_MODEL environment variable only when explicitly
207		// configured. When not configured, the Gemini CLI uses its built-in default model.
208		// This avoids embedding the value directly in the shell command (which fails template injection
209		// validation for GitHub Actions expressions like ${{ inputs.model }}).
210		modelConfigured := workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Model != ""
211	
212		// Gemini CLI reads MCP config from .gemini/settings.json (project-level)
213		// The conversion script (convert_gateway_config_gemini.sh) writes settings.json
214		// during the MCP setup step, so no --mcp-config flag is needed here.
215	
216		// Auto-approve all tool executions (equivalent to Codex's --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox)
217		// Without this, Gemini CLI's default approval mode rejects tool calls with "Tool execution denied by policy"
218		geminiArgs = append(geminiArgs, "--yolo")
219	
220		// Add streaming JSON output (JSONL format, compatible with the log parser)
221		geminiArgs = append(geminiArgs, "--output-format", "stream-json")
222	
223		// Note: the --prompt argument is appended raw after shellJoinArgs below because it contains
224		// a shell command substitution ("$(cat ...)") that must NOT go through shellEscapeArg —
225		// single-quoting it would prevent shell expansion at runtime.
226	
227		// Build the command
228		commandName := "gemini"
229		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Command != "" {
230			commandName = workflowData.EngineConfig.Command
231		}
232	
233		// Append the prompt arg raw (not through shellJoinArgs) to preserve shell expansion
234		geminiCommand := fmt.Sprintf(`%s %s --prompt "$(cat /tmp/gh-aw/aw-prompts/prompt.txt)"`, commandName, shellJoinArgs(geminiArgs))
235	
236		// Build the full command with AWF wrapping if enabled
237		var command string
238		firewallEnabled := isFirewallEnabled(workflowData)
239		if firewallEnabled {
240			allowedDomains := GetGeminiAllowedDomainsWithToolsAndRuntimes(
241				workflowData.NetworkPermissions,
242				workflowData.Tools,
243				workflowData.Runtimes,
244			)
245			// Add GHES/custom API target domains to the firewall allow-list when engine.api-target is set
246			if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.APITarget != "" {
247				allowedDomains = mergeAPITargetDomains(allowedDomains, workflowData.EngineConfig.APITarget)
248			}
249	
250			npmPathSetup := GetNpmBinPathSetup()
251			geminiCommandWithPath := fmt.Sprintf("%s && %s", npmPathSetup, geminiCommand)
252	
253			command = BuildAWFCommand(AWFCommandConfig{
254				EngineName:     "gemini",
255				EngineCommand:  geminiCommandWithPath,
256				LogFile:        logFile,
257				WorkflowData:   workflowData,
258				UsesTTY:        false,
259				AllowedDomains: allowedDomains,
260				// Create the agent step summary file before AWF starts so it is accessible
261				// inside the sandbox. The agent writes its step summary content here, and the
262				// file is appended to $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY after secret redaction.
263				PathSetup: "touch " + AgentStepSummaryPath,
264				// Exclude every env var whose step-env value is a secret so the agent
265				// cannot read raw token values via bash tools (env / printenv).
266				ExcludeEnvVarNames: ComputeAWFExcludeEnvVarNames(workflowData, []string{"GEMINI_API_KEY"}),
267			})
268		} else {
269			command = fmt.Sprintf(`set -o pipefail
270	touch %s
271	%s 2>&1 | tee -a %s`, AgentStepSummaryPath, geminiCommand, logFile)
272		}
273	
274		// Build environment variables
275		env := map[string]string{
276			"GEMINI_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.GEMINI_API_KEY }}",
277			"GH_AW_PROMPT":   "/tmp/gh-aw/aw-prompts/prompt.txt",
278			// Tag the step as a GitHub AW agentic execution for discoverability by agents
279			"GITHUB_AW":        "true",
280			"GITHUB_WORKSPACE": "${{ github.workspace }}",
281			// Override GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY with a path that exists inside the sandbox.
282			// The runner's original path is unreachable within the AWF isolated filesystem;
283			// we create this file before the agent starts and append it to the real
284			// $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY after secret redaction.
285			"GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY": AgentStepSummaryPath,
286			// Enable verbose debug logging from Gemini CLI for better diagnostics.
287			// Gemini CLI uses the npm 'debug' package, and 'gemini-cli:*' enables all
288			// internal Gemini CLI debug channels (see: https://gemini-cli-docs.pages.dev/cli/configuration).
289			// Non-JSON debug lines are gracefully skipped by ParseLogMetrics.
290			"DEBUG": "gemini-cli:*",
291		}
292		// Indicate the phase: "agent" for the main run, "detection" for threat detection
293		// Include the compiler version so agents can identify which gh-aw version generated the workflow
294		if workflowData.IsDetectionRun {
295			env["GH_AW_PHASE"] = "detection"
296		} else {
297			env["GH_AW_PHASE"] = "agent"
298		}
299		if IsRelease() {
300			env["GH_AW_VERSION"] = GetVersion()
301		} else {
302			env["GH_AW_VERSION"] = "dev"
303		}
304	
305		// Add MCP config env var if needed (points to .gemini/settings.json for Gemini)
306		if HasMCPServers(workflowData) {
307			env["GH_AW_MCP_CONFIG"] = "${{ github.workspace }}/.gemini/settings.json"
308		}
309	
310		// When the firewall (AWF) is enabled with --enable-api-proxy, point Gemini CLI at the
311		// LLM gateway sidecar instead of the real googleapis.com endpoint.
312		if firewallEnabled {
313			env["GEMINI_API_BASE_URL"] = fmt.Sprintf("http://host.docker.internal:%d", constants.GeminiLLMGatewayPort)
314	
315			// Set git identity environment variables so the first git commit succeeds inside the
316			// container. AWF's --env-all forwards these to the container, ensuring git does not
317			// rely on the host-side ~/.gitconfig which is not visible in the sandbox.
318			maps.Copy(env, getGitIdentityEnvVars())
319		}
320	
321		// Add safe outputs env
322		applySafeOutputEnvToMap(env, workflowData)
323	
324		// Set the model environment variable only when explicitly configured.
325		// When model is configured, use the native GEMINI_MODEL env var - the Gemini CLI reads it
326		// directly, avoiding the need to embed the value in the shell command (which would fail
327		// template injection validation for GitHub Actions expressions like ${{ inputs.model }}).
328		// When model is not configured, let the Gemini CLI use its built-in default model.
329		if modelConfigured {
330			geminiLog.Printf("Setting %s env var for model: %s", constants.GeminiCLIModelEnvVar, workflowData.EngineConfig.Model)
331			env[constants.GeminiCLIModelEnvVar] = workflowData.EngineConfig.Model
332		}
333	
334		// Add custom environment variables from engine config.
335		// This allows users to override the default engine token expression (e.g.
336		// GEMINI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.MY_ORG_GEMINI_KEY }}) via engine.env.
337		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && len(workflowData.EngineConfig.Env) > 0 {
338			maps.Copy(env, workflowData.EngineConfig.Env)
339		}
340	
341		// Add custom environment variables from agent config
342		agentConfig := getAgentConfig(workflowData)
343		if agentConfig != nil && len(agentConfig.Env) > 0 {
344			maps.Copy(env, agentConfig.Env)
345			geminiLog.Printf("Added %d custom env vars from agent config", len(agentConfig.Env))
346		}
347	
348		// Generate the execution step
349		stepLines := []string{
350			"      - name: Execute Gemini CLI",
351			"        id: agentic_execution",
352		}
353	
354		// Filter environment variables for security
355		allowedSecrets := e.GetRequiredSecretNames(workflowData)
356		filteredEnv := FilterEnvForSecrets(env, allowedSecrets)
357	
358		// Format step with command and env
359		stepLines = FormatStepWithCommandAndEnv(stepLines, command, filteredEnv)
360	
361		steps = append(steps, GitHubActionStep(stepLines))
362		return steps
363	}
364

1	// This file provides shared helper functions for AI engine implementations.
2	//
3	// This file contains utilities used across multiple AI engine files (copilot_engine.go,
4	// claude_engine.go, codex_engine.go, custom_engine.go) to generate common workflow
5	// steps and configurations.
6	//
7	// # Organization Rationale
8	//
9	// These helper functions are grouped here because they:
10	//   - Are used by 3+ engine implementations (shared utilities)
11	//   - Provide common patterns for agent installation and npm setup
12	//   - Have a clear domain focus (engine workflow generation)
13	//   - Are stable and change infrequently
14	//
15	// This follows the helper file conventions documented in skills/developer/SKILL.md.
16	//
17	// # Key Functions
18	//
19	// Agent Installation:
20	//   - GenerateAgentInstallSteps() - Generate agent installation workflow steps
21	//
22	// NPM Installation:
23	//   - GenerateNpmInstallStep() - Generate npm package installation step
24	//   - GenerateEngineDependenciesInstallStep() - Generate engine dependencies install step
25	//
26	// Configuration:
27	//   - GetClaudeSystemPrompt() - Get system prompt for Claude engine
28	//
29	// These functions encapsulate shared logic that would otherwise be duplicated across
30	// engine files, maintaining DRY principles while keeping engine-specific code separate.
31	
32	package workflow
33	
34	import (
35		"fmt"
36		"regexp"
37		"sort"
38		"strings"
39	
40		"github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/logger"
41	)
42	
43	var engineHelpersLog = logger.New("workflow:engine_helpers")
44	
45	// agentFilePathRegex is an allowlist of safe characters for agent file paths.
46	// Only alphanumeric characters, dots, underscores, hyphens, forward slashes, and
47	// spaces are permitted. This prevents shell metacharacters (such as ", $, `, ;, |)
48	// from being embedded in paths that are later interpolated into shell commands.
49	var agentFilePathRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9._/\- ]+$`)
50	
51	// EngineInstallConfig contains configuration for engine installation steps.
52	// This struct centralizes the configuration needed to generate the common
53	// installation steps shared by all engines (secret validation and npm installation).
54	type EngineInstallConfig struct {
55		// Secrets is a list of secret names to validate (at least one must be set)
56		Secrets []string
57		// DocsURL is the documentation URL shown when secret validation fails
58		DocsURL string
59		// NpmPackage is the npm package name (e.g., "@github/copilot")
60		NpmPackage string
61		// Version is the default version of the npm package
62		Version string
63		// Name is the engine display name for secret validation messages (e.g., "Claude Code")
64		Name string
65		// CliName is the CLI name used for cache key prefix (e.g., "copilot")
66		CliName string
67		// InstallStepName is the display name for the npm install step (e.g., "Install Claude Code CLI")
68		InstallStepName string
69	}
70	
71	// getEngineEnvOverrides returns the engine.env map from workflowData, or nil if not set.
72	// This is used to pass user-provided env overrides to steps such as secret validation,
73	// so that overridden token expressions are used instead of the default "${{ secrets.KEY }}".
74	func getEngineEnvOverrides(workflowData *WorkflowData) map[string]string {
75		if workflowData == nil || workflowData.EngineConfig == nil {
76			return nil
77		}
78		return workflowData.EngineConfig.Env
79	}
80	
81	// GetBaseInstallationSteps returns the common installation steps for an engine.
82	// This includes npm package installation steps shared across all engines.
83	// Secret validation is now handled in the activation job via GetSecretValidationStep.
84	//
85	// Parameters:
86	//   - config: Engine-specific configuration for installation
87	//   - workflowData: The workflow data containing engine configuration
88	//
89	// Returns:
90	//   - []GitHubActionStep: The base installation steps (npm install)
91	func GetBaseInstallationSteps(config EngineInstallConfig, workflowData *WorkflowData) []GitHubActionStep {
92		engineHelpersLog.Printf("Generating base installation steps for %s engine: workflow=%s", config.Name, workflowData.Name)
93	
94		var steps []GitHubActionStep
95	
96		// Secret validation step is now generated in the activation job (GetSecretValidationStep).
97	
98		// Determine step name - use InstallStepName if provided, otherwise default to "Install <Name>"
99		stepName := config.InstallStepName
100		if stepName == "" {
101			stepName = "Install " + config.Name
102		}
103	
104		// Add npm package installation steps
105		npmSteps := BuildStandardNpmEngineInstallSteps(
106			config.NpmPackage,
107			config.Version,
108			stepName,
109			config.CliName,
110			workflowData,
111		)
112		steps = append(steps, npmSteps...)
113	
114		return steps
115	}
116	
117	// ResolveAgentFilePath validates and returns the properly quoted agent file path with GITHUB_WORKSPACE prefix.
118	// This helper extracts the common pattern shared by Copilot, Codex, and Claude engines.
119	//
120	// The agent file path is relative to the repository root, so we prefix it with ${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}
121	// and wrap the entire expression in double quotes to handle paths with spaces while allowing
122	// shell variable expansion.
123	//
124	// Parameters:
125	//   - agentFile: The relative path to the agent file (e.g., ".github/agents/test-agent.md")
126	//
127	// Returns:
128	//   - string: The double-quoted path with GITHUB_WORKSPACE prefix (e.g., "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/.github/agents/test-agent.md")
129	//   - error: Non-nil if agentFile contains characters that could cause shell injection
130	//
131	// The path is validated against an allowlist of safe characters before returning. Only
132	// alphanumeric characters, dots, underscores, hyphens, forward slashes, and spaces are
133	// permitted. Shell metacharacters (", $, `, ;, |, \, etc.) are rejected to prevent injection.
134	//
135	// Example:
136	//
137	//	agentPath, err := ResolveAgentFilePath(".github/agents/my-agent.md")
138	//	// Returns: "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/.github/agents/my-agent.md", nil
139	func ResolveAgentFilePath(agentFile string) (string, error) {
140		if !agentFilePathRegex.MatchString(agentFile) {
141			return "", fmt.Errorf("agent file path contains invalid characters: %q (only alphanumeric characters, dots, underscores, hyphens, forward slashes, and spaces are allowed)", agentFile)
142		}
143		return fmt.Sprintf("\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/%s\"", agentFile), nil
144	}
145	
146	// BuildStandardNpmEngineInstallSteps creates standard npm installation steps for engines
147	// This helper extracts the common pattern shared by Copilot, Codex, and Claude engines.
148	//
149	// Parameters:
150	//   - packageName: The npm package name (e.g., "@github/copilot")
151	//   - defaultVersion: The default version constant (e.g., constants.DefaultCopilotVersion)
152	//   - stepName: The display name for the install step (e.g., "Install GitHub Copilot CLI")
153	//   - cacheKeyPrefix: The cache key prefix (e.g., "copilot")
154	//   - workflowData: The workflow data containing engine configuration
155	//
156	// Returns:
157	//   - []GitHubActionStep: The installation steps including Node.js setup
158	func BuildStandardNpmEngineInstallSteps(
159		packageName string,
160		defaultVersion string,
161		stepName string,
162		cacheKeyPrefix string,
163		workflowData *WorkflowData,
164	) []GitHubActionStep {
165		engineHelpersLog.Printf("Building npm engine install steps: package=%s, version=%s", packageName, defaultVersion)
166	
167		// Use version from engine config if provided, otherwise default to pinned version
168		version := defaultVersion
169		if workflowData.EngineConfig != nil && workflowData.EngineConfig.Version != "" {
170			version = workflowData.EngineConfig.Version
171			engineHelpersLog.Printf("Using engine config version: %s", version)
172		}
173	
174		// Add npm package installation steps (includes Node.js setup)
175		return GenerateNpmInstallSteps(
176			packageName,
177			version,
178			stepName,
179			cacheKeyPrefix,
180			true, // Include Node.js setup
181		)
182	}
183	
184	// RenderCustomMCPToolConfigHandler is a function type that engines must provide to render their specific MCP config
185	// FormatStepWithCommandAndEnv formats a GitHub Actions step with command and environment variables.
186	// This shared function extracts the common pattern used by Copilot and Codex engines.
187	//
188	// Parameters:
189	//   - stepLines: Existing step lines to append to (e.g., name, id, comments, timeout)
190	//   - command: The command to execute (may contain multiple lines)
191	//   - env: Map of environment variables to include in the step
192	//
193	// Returns:
194	//   - []string: Complete step lines including run command and env section
195	func FormatStepWithCommandAndEnv(stepLines []string, command string, env map[string]string) []string {
196		engineHelpersLog.Printf("Formatting step with command and %d environment variables", len(env))
197		// Add the run section
198		stepLines = append(stepLines, "        run: |")
199	
200		// Split command into lines and indent them properly
201		commandLines := strings.SplitSeq(command, "\n")
202		for line := range commandLines {
203			// Don't add indentation to empty lines
204			if line == "" {
205				stepLines = append(stepLines, "")
206			} else {
207				stepLines = append(stepLines, "          "+line)
208			}
209		}
210	
211		// Add environment variables
212		if len(env) > 0 {
213			stepLines = append(stepLines, "        env:")
214			// Sort environment keys for consistent output
215			envKeys := make([]string, 0, len(env))
216			for key := range env {
217				envKeys = append(envKeys, key)
218			}
219			sort.Strings(envKeys)
220	
221			for _, key := range envKeys {
222				value := env[key]
223				stepLines = append(stepLines, fmt.Sprintf("          %s: %s", key, yamlStringValue(value)))
224			}
225		}
226	
227		return stepLines
228	}
229	
230	// yamlStringValue returns a YAML-safe representation of a string value.
231	// If the value starts with a YAML flow indicator ('{' or '[') or other characters
232	// that would cause it to be misinterpreted by YAML parsers, it wraps the value
233	// in single quotes. Any embedded single quotes are escaped by doubling them (' becomes ”).
234	func yamlStringValue(value string) string {
235		if len(value) == 0 {
236			return value
237		}
238		// Values starting with YAML flow indicators need quoting to be treated as strings.
239		// '{' would be parsed as a YAML flow mapping, '[' as a YAML flow sequence.
240		first := value[0]
241		if first != '{' && first != '[' {
242			return value
243		}
244		// Single-quote the value, escaping any embedded single quotes by doubling them.
245		return "'" + strings.ReplaceAll(value, "'", "''") + "'"
246	}
247	
248	// FilterEnvForSecrets filters environment variables to only include allowed secrets.
249	// This is a security measure to ensure that only necessary secrets are passed to the execution step.
250	//
251	// An env var carrying a secret reference is kept when either:
252	//   - The referenced secret name (e.g. "COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN") is in allowedNamesAndKeys, OR
253	//   - The env var key itself (e.g. "COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN") is in allowedNamesAndKeys.
254	//
255	// The second rule allows users to override an engine's required env var with a
256	// differently-named secret, e.g. COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.MY_ORG_TOKEN }}.
257	//
258	// Parameters:
259	//   - env: Map of all environment variables
260	//   - allowedNamesAndKeys: List of secret names and/or env var keys that are permitted
261	//
262	// Returns:
263	//   - map[string]string: Filtered environment variables with only allowed secrets
264	func FilterEnvForSecrets(env map[string]string, allowedNamesAndKeys []string) map[string]string {
265		engineHelpersLog.Printf("Filtering environment variables: total=%d, allowed=%d", len(env), len(allowedNamesAndKeys))
266	
267		// Create a set for fast lookup — entries may be secret names or env var keys.
268		allowedSet := make(map[string]bool)
269		for _, entry := range allowedNamesAndKeys {
270			allowedSet[entry] = true
271		}
272	
273		filtered := make(map[string]string)
274		secretsRemoved := 0
275	
276		for key, value := range env {
277			// Check if this env var is a secret reference (starts with "${{ secrets.")
278			if strings.Contains(value, "${{ secrets.") {
279				// Extract the secret name from the expression
280				// Format: ${{ secrets.SECRET_NAME }} or ${{ secrets.SECRET_NAME || ... }}
281				secretName := ExtractSecretName(value)
282				// Allow the secret if the secret name OR the env var key is in the allowed set.
283				if secretName != "" && !allowedSet[secretName] && !allowedSet[key] {
284					engineHelpersLog.Printf("Removing unauthorized secret from env: %s (secret: %s)", key, secretName)
285					secretsRemoved++
286					continue
287				}
288			}
289			filtered[key] = value
290		}
291	
292		engineHelpersLog.Printf("Filtered environment variables: kept=%d, removed=%d", len(filtered), secretsRemoved)
293		return filtered
294	}
295	
296	// GetNpmBinPathSetup returns a simple shell command that adds hostedtoolcache bin directories
297	// to PATH. This is specifically for npm-installed CLIs (like Claude and Codex) that need
298	// to find their binaries installed via `npm install -g`.
299	//
300	// Unlike GetHostedToolcachePathSetup(), this does NOT use GH_AW_TOOL_BINS because AWF's
301	// native chroot mode already handles tool-specific paths (GOROOT, JAVA_HOME, etc.) via
302	// AWF_HOST_PATH and the entrypoint.sh script. This function only adds the generic
303	// hostedtoolcache bin directories for npm packages.
304	//
305	// Returns:
306	//   - string: A shell command that exports PATH with hostedtoolcache bin directories prepended
307	func GetNpmBinPathSetup() string {
308		// Find all bin directories in hostedtoolcache (Node.js, Python, etc.)
309		// This finds paths like /opt/hostedtoolcache/node/22.13.0/x64/bin
310		//
311		// After the find, re-prepend GOROOT/bin if set. The find returns directories
312		// alphabetically, so go/1.23.12 shadows go/1.25.0. Re-prepending GOROOT/bin
313		// ensures the Go version set by actions/setup-go takes precedence.
314		// AWF's entrypoint.sh exports GOROOT before the user command runs.
315		return `export PATH="$(find /opt/hostedtoolcache -maxdepth 4 -type d -name bin 2>/dev/null | tr '\n' ':')$PATH"; [ -n "$GOROOT" ] && export PATH="$GOROOT/bin:$PATH" || true`
316	}
317	
318	// EngineHasValidateSecretStep checks if the engine provides a validate-secret step.
319	// This is used to determine whether the secret_verification_result job output should be added.
320	//
321	// The validate-secret step is provided by engines that override GetSecretValidationStep():
322	//   - Copilot engine: Adds step unless copilot-requests feature is enabled or custom command is set
323	//   - Claude engine: Adds step unless custom command is set
324	//   - Codex engine: Adds step unless custom command is set
325	//   - Gemini engine: Adds step unless custom command is set
326	//   - Custom engine: Never adds this step (uses BaseEngine default which returns empty)
327	//
328	// Parameters:
329	//   - engine: The agentic engine to check
330	//   - data: The workflow data (needed for GetSecretValidationStep)
331	//
332	// Returns:
333	//   - bool: true if the engine provides a validate-secret step, false otherwise
334	func EngineHasValidateSecretStep(engine CodingAgentEngine, data *WorkflowData) bool {
335		step := engine.GetSecretValidationStep(data)
336		return len(step) > 0
337	}

339	// BuildInvalidAgentPathStep returns a single-step slice that immediately exits non-zero
340	// with a human-readable error message. Use this when ResolveAgentFilePath rejects an
341	// agent file path at step-generation time so that the compiled workflow fails clearly
342	// rather than silently skipping the engine execution step.
343	//
344	// Parameters:
345	//   - stepName: The step name to display (e.g. "Execute Codex CLI")
346	//   - agentFile: The invalid agent file path provided by the user
347	//   - err: The validation error returned by ResolveAgentFilePath
348	//
349	// Returns:
350	//   - []GitHubActionStep: A slice containing a single failing step
351	func BuildInvalidAgentPathStep(stepName, agentFile string, err error) []GitHubActionStep {
352		errMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Invalid agent file path %q: %v", agentFile, err)
353		engineHelpersLog.Printf("Building invalid agent path step: %s", errMsg)
354		var stepLines []string
355		stepLines = append(stepLines, "      - name: "+stepName)
356		stepLines = append(stepLines, "        run: |")
357		stepLines = append(stepLines, fmt.Sprintf("          echo 'Error: %s' >&2", strings.ReplaceAll(errMsg, "'", "'\\''")))
358		stepLines = append(stepLines, "          exit 1")
359		return []GitHubActionStep{GitHubActionStep(stepLines)}
360	}

10	// GenerateCopilotInstallerSteps creates GitHub Actions steps to install the Copilot CLI using the official installer.
11	func GenerateCopilotInstallerSteps(version, stepName string) []GitHubActionStep {
12		// If no version is specified, use the default version from constants.
13		// "latest" means the installer will use the latest available release.
14		if version == "" {
15			version = string(constants.DefaultCopilotVersion)
16			copilotInstallerLog.Printf("No version specified, using default: %s", version)
17		}
18	
19		copilotInstallerLog.Printf("Generating Copilot installer steps using install_copilot_cli.sh: version=%s", version)
20	
21		// Use the install_copilot_cli.sh script from actions/setup/sh
22		// This script includes retry logic for robustness against transient network failures.
23		// The script downloads the Copilot CLI using curl with hardcoded github.com URLs
24		// and does not use gh CLI, so GH_HOST does not affect the download. No step-level
25		// GH_HOST override is needed here; the correct host is already set in GITHUB_ENV
26		// by configure_gh_for_ghe.sh (or by the Derive GH_HOST step when DIFC proxy is active).
27		stepLines := []string{
28			"      - name: " + stepName,
29			"        run: ${RUNNER_TEMP}/gh-aw/actions/install_copilot_cli.sh " + version,
30		}
31	
32		return []GitHubActionStep{GitHubActionStep(stepLines)}
33	}

10	var nodejsLog = logger.New("workflow:nodejs")
11	
12	// GenerateNodeJsSetupStep creates a GitHub Actions step for setting up Node.js
13	// Returns a step that installs Node.js using the default version from constants.DefaultNodeVersion
14	// Caching is disabled by default to prevent cache poisoning vulnerabilities in release workflows
15	func GenerateNodeJsSetupStep() GitHubActionStep {
16		return GitHubActionStep{
17			"      - name: Setup Node.js",
18			"        uses: " + GetActionPin("actions/setup-node"),
19			"        with:",
20			fmt.Sprintf("          node-version: '%s'", constants.DefaultNodeVersion),
21			"          package-manager-cache: false",
22		}
23	}
24	
25	// GenerateNpmInstallSteps creates GitHub Actions steps for installing an npm package globally
26	// Parameters:
27	//   - packageName: The npm package name (e.g., "@anthropic-ai/claude-code")
28	//   - version: The package version to install
29	//   - stepName: The name to display for the install step (e.g., "Install Claude Code CLI")
30	//   - cacheKeyPrefix: The prefix for the cache key (unused, kept for API compatibility)
31	//   - includeNodeSetup: If true, includes Node.js setup step before npm install
32	//
33	// Returns steps for installing the npm package (optionally with Node.js setup)
34	func GenerateNpmInstallSteps(packageName, version, stepName, cacheKeyPrefix string, includeNodeSetup bool) []GitHubActionStep {
35		return GenerateNpmInstallStepsWithScope(packageName, version, stepName, cacheKeyPrefix, includeNodeSetup, true)
36	}
37	
38	// GenerateNpmInstallStepsWithScope generates npm installation steps with control over global vs local installation
39	func GenerateNpmInstallStepsWithScope(packageName, version, stepName, cacheKeyPrefix string, includeNodeSetup bool, isGlobal bool) []GitHubActionStep {
40		nodejsLog.Printf("Generating npm install steps: package=%s, version=%s, includeNodeSetup=%v, isGlobal=%v", packageName, version, includeNodeSetup, isGlobal)
41	
42		var steps []GitHubActionStep
43	
44		// Add Node.js setup if requested
45		if includeNodeSetup {
46			nodejsLog.Print("Including Node.js setup step")
47			steps = append(steps, GenerateNodeJsSetupStep())
48		}
49	
50		// Add npm install step
51		globalFlag := ""
52		if isGlobal {
53			globalFlag = "-g "
54		}
55		installCmd := fmt.Sprintf("npm install %s%s@%s", globalFlag, packageName, version)
56		steps = append(steps, GitHubActionStep{
57			"      - name: " + stepName,
58			"        run: " + installCmd,
59		})
60	
61		return steps
62	}
63

content.text:
Implemented the fix for `engine.version` GitHub Actions expressions with injection-safe handling.

Summary:
- Updated npm engine installation generation (`pkg/workflow/nodejs.go`) so whole GitHub Actions expressions like `${{ inputs.engine-version }}` are passed through `ENGINE_VERSION` env var and referenced as `"$ENGINE_VERSION"` in the shell command.
- Updated Copilot installer generation (`pkg/workflow/copilot_installer.go`) with the same env-var based handling.
- Added shared whole-expression detection helper in `pkg/workflow/dispatch_repository_validation.go`.
- Updated JSON schema descriptions/examples for `engine.version` and inline `runtime.version` to document GitHub Actions expression support.
- Verified literal versions such as `1.2.3` remain inline/backward-compatible.
- Wrote temporary regression tests to reproduce the failure, confirmed they failed before the fix, confirmed they passed after the fix, then deleted them as requested.

Validation run:
```bash
go test ./pkg/workflow -run 'TestGenerateCopilotInstallerSteps|TestCopilotInstallerCustomVersion|TestBuildStandardNpmEngineInstallSteps'
```

Result:
```text
ok  	github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/workflow
```

Notes:
- I did not commit any changes.
- Existing unrelated working tree changes were present and left untouched:
  - `.github/agents/agentic-workflows.agent.md`
  - `reports/` untracked directory.
